intro: Ditassa semirii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ditassa semirii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Ditassa semirii include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and digestive systems.
